top30.title: Pretraining a foundation model for small-molecule natural products
top30.abstract: Nature Machine Intelligence, Published online: 29 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s42256-026-01226-8Ding et al. present a scaffold-aware foundation model for small-molecule natural products leveraging masked objectives and contrastive learning to enhance taxonomy classification, genome mining and virtual screening in drug discovery.

top30.title: AI scientist agents violate research integrity rules
top30.abstract: Science, Volume 392, Issue 6798, Page 569-569, May 2026.

top30.title: Force-free molecular dynamics through autoregressive equivariant networks
top30.abstract: Nature Machine Intelligence, Published online: 05 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s42256-026-01227-7Thiemann et al. introduce TrajCast, a neural network that bypasses force calculations to directly predict atomic trajectories, enabling time steps up to 30 times longer while accurately reproducing physical properties of molecules and materials.

top30.title: Merging the classical and the modern in physics-based simulations
top30.abstract: Nature Computational Science, Published online: 28 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s43588-026-00978-yEngineering simulations traditionally rely on finite element methods, which are accurate but computationally expensive, while scientific machine learning offers faster, data-driven alternatives. The recently developed neural-operator element method combines both approaches, making simulations more efficient and scalable.

top30.title: Call your AI agent
top30.abstract: Nature Methods, Published online: 01 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41592-026-03088-9AI agent systems are now being built on top of large language models, and they can conduct autonomous analyses. Choosing among the offerings takes a balance of curiosity, excitement and skepticism.

top30.title: Molecular skeleton programming of premediators in sulfur electrochemistry
top30.abstract: Nature, Published online: 06 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10505-8A potential premediator, 2-chloropyrimidine, could be a model material for molecular skeleton design enabling lithium–sulfur batteries to achieve a strong average capacity retention and help design functional molecules in broader organic chemical spaces.

top30.title: SmileyLlama: modifying large language models for directed chemical space exploration
top30.abstract: Nature Computational Science, Published online: 11 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s43588-026-00986-yA general-purpose large language model can be trained to create a specialized model, SmileyLlama, that can generate drug-like molecules with user-specified properties that are relevant to medicinal chemistry through a process that might be extended to other scientific domains.

top30.title: Publisher Correction: Presymptomatic training mitigates functional deficits in a mouse model of Rett syndrome
top30.abstract: Nature, Published online: 11 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10578-5Publisher Correction: Presymptomatic training mitigates functional deficits in a mouse model of Rett syndrome

top30.title: Audio long read: The air is full of DNA — here’s what scientists are using it for
top30.abstract: Nature, Published online: 11 May 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01522-8Floating genetic material could give insights into ecosystem health, the presence of invasive species, and help researchers identify pathogens before they cause harm.

top30.title: The sleep paradox: why do humans sleep so little when we need it so much?
top30.abstract: Nature, Published online: 11 May 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01478-9A new book brings together ethnography, neurobiology and primatology to argue that how much our species sleeps is an evolutionary trade-off, with lessons for how each of us can sleep better.

top30.title: Elsevier vs Meta: first science publisher sues over scraped research papers
top30.abstract: Nature, Published online: 11 May 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01481-0Science publishing giant Elsevier has joined a class-action lawsuit against Meta that alleges the reproduction of copyrighted works in developing the Llama AI model.

top30.title: Best. Day. Ever. What does a good day in science look like?
top30.abstract: Nature, Published online: 11 May 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01479-8Struggling to remember why you picked a career in science? Here are some heart-warming tales to jog your memory.

top30.title: How a passion for baking fermented a fresh career move

top30.title: ‘Undruggable’ cancer proteins meet their match

top30.abstract: Nature, Published online: 07 May 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01466-zAnalysis of papers from millions of scientists shows that older researchers tend to stick with ideas from their past.
